strat: Columbus Crew, under interim head coach Laurent Courtois, has reverted to a familiar 5-2-3 (or 3-4-2-1) formation, emphasizing a strong defensive press and fluid attacking transitions, a style that brought them success under former coach Wilfried Nancy. They aim for more direct play and aggressive attacks, focusing on frustrating and overloading opponents. Burnley, recently relegated to the Championship, is in a transitional period with new manager Nicky Hayen, who was appointed on July 10, 2026. Their pre-season began with a 3-1 loss to FC Cincinnati, where they heavily rotated their squad. Historically, Burnley under previous management favored a defensive 4-2-3-1 or a five-man defense, focusing on a low block and direct long balls, but Hayen's tactical imprint is still developing.
injuries: For Columbus Crew, key striker Wessam Abou Ali is out for the remainder of the 2026 season due to a torn ACL and meniscus. Defender Malte Amundsen is sidelined with a thigh injury, and forward Jamal Thiare is questionable with a calf injury. Burnley will be without central midfielder Josh Cullen, who is recovering from a cruciate ligament tear and is expected back in early September 2026. Defender Jordan Beyer is also out with a knee injury, anticipated to return in early August 2026. Jaydon Banel is also listed as injured.
ref: No specific referee has been announced for this international club friendly match. In such pre-season fixtures, referees typically adopt a more lenient approach, allowing for a free-flowing game with fewer stoppages. The emphasis is usually on player fitness and tactical experimentation rather than strict enforcement of every minor infraction. However, player safety and major fouls will still be addressed decisively.
tip: Columbus Crew is favored to win this friendly. They enter the match with better momentum, having secured two consecutive competitive victories under interim coach Laurent Courtois, who has successfully re-established their effective 5-2-3 system. Burnley, in contrast, is undergoing a managerial transition and showed vulnerability in their recent 3-1 pre-season defeat to FC Cincinnati. While both teams are expected to rotate players, Columbus's home advantage and desire to build confidence before the MLS season resumes should give them the edge against a Burnley side still finding its footing under new leadership.
prob: Columbus Crew has an approximate 50-55% chance of winning this match, with expected odds around 1.80-2.00. A draw is estimated at 25-30%, and a Burnley win at 15-20%. A specific correct score prediction of 2-1 in favor of Columbus Crew has been suggested, and 'both teams to score' is considered a strong betting option for this friendly.
